title: VerificationAnswerProposal
description: A verifier-sourced proposal answering an upper-dialect open question (a Description with is_question=True, in descriptions-01 terms), per design/verification-sketch-01.md V2 + PFS10-AP1..AP3. Parallels AnswerProposal from reader-model-01 but the proposer is a verifier, not a reader-model partner. question_id uses inline $defs/cross_dialect_ref per PFS10-X1. status is an open non-empty string per PFS10-X3; a close-enum awaits the walker acceptance flow (OQ3).

cross_dialect_ref.description: Typed reference to a record in a specific dialect. Re-inlined per PFS8-V + PFS10-X1. Open dialect-token string per PFS9-X4.

record_id.description: Stable identifier for the referenced record in its dialect.

proposer_id.description: Stable identifier for the verifier proposing the answer. PFS10-AP2.

question_id.description: CrossDialectRef pointing at the is_question=True Description the proposal answers. PFS10-AP2.

rationale.description: Prose explaining why the verifier proposes this answer — grounded in the substrate / upper-dialect records the verifier read. PFS10-AP2.

proposed_at_τ_a.description: Author-time τ_a at which the proposal was emitted. PFS10-AP2.

status.description: Open non-empty string per PFS10-AP3 + PFS10-X3. Today's Python hard-codes initial value 'pending'; the walker acceptance flow (deferred per verification-sketch-01 §Deferred) would mutate to 'accepted' / 'rejected' / etc. Close-enum deferred to PFS10 OQ3.
